channel.title: Comments for page "Contact Staff"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Contact Staff"

item.title: Request
item.content:encoded: Good day.Can I create a regular page for our translation team?Please reply as soon as possible.(｡>∀<｡)

item.title: Re: Update
item.content:encoded: I like this new update. The page looks more better, at my opinion.

item.title: Re: Update
item.content:encoded: Huzzah! Thank you McB and TSAT!

item.title: Update
item.content:encoded:
I've posted a much needed update to this page.

Shout out to TSAT for making the new table of contents not look god awful anymore!

item.title: Moderator Post - Extremely Closed. The MOST Closed.
item.content:encoded:
Stop posting on this thread. You are posting non-relevant, contentless comments on a thread for a specific page on the wiki.

This is a CLOSED STAFF POST. Stop responding.

item.title: flamingoś
item.content:encoded:
whoever did the flamongoś SCP story thing did a great job, one of the best scenarioś ive ever read
and staff, have you seen the scp i have written i called it SCP-8384 but im not sure if that number of scp is in use or not, can you please check it for me

item.title: Mod Post - EXTRA Closed
item.content:encoded:
Cease this line of discussion.

This is a CLOSED staff post. Do not reply unless you are staff.

item.title: and i didnt know
item.content:encoded: [removed by staff]

item.title: im not jokeing
item.content:encoded: [removed by staff]

item.title: Mod Post- Closed
item.content:encoded:
Further behavior along this line will get you banned from this site. The SCP Foundation is fictional.

DO NOT REPLY TO CLOSED STAFFPOSTS.

item.title: MTF dispatch
item.content:encoded: [Removed by staff]

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Gotcha, thank you; I'll try to contact them about it again and see if I can get a more timely response this time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I believe that's a wikidot issue then. We've had reports of other site members encountering the same login problem.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes to both, plus I've tried several different browsers by this point, both mobile and desktop; the only consistent factor seems to be whether I'm using scp-wiki.net or scp-wiki.wikidot.com, hence why I thought it might be a problem specifically with the configuration of scp-wiki.net.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
We have a technical team who might be able to help with this, but that sounds like a wikidot error.

Do you have cookies and JavaScript enabled for the site? If not, that might explain why your login keeps expiring.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is there staff to whom technical issues should be submitted, or is that purely a Wikidot Problem? I've been having an issue for years which has been driving me up the wall wherein I can log in to scp-wiki.wikidot.com, but scp-wiki.net will not let me log in. It will go through the login screen but just spit me back out without logging me in. I have to manually navigate to the wikidot page in order to comment or upvote. I've contacted Wikidot about it, but they seemed.. unhelpful… and since it seems to be a specific issue with the scp-wiki.net site, I thought perhaps it was the site's purview itself. But no staff are listed for site issues that I can see.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I believe the subforum's name was changed, so the link broke. It should be fixed now; thanks for bringing it to staff attention!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It seems that the Policy subforum does not exist.What happened?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good catch—I put that info under a collapsible. The current applications systems is still effectively a trial run, so that info will stay on the page.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Shouldn't the application part be removed due to the process being changed?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Issue fixed, nothing to see here.

item.title: Re: New Guide!
item.content:encoded: Yeah, the table of contents is bugging me too, but it's an automatically-generated thing so I don't know if bullet points can be added. :/ Spacing is a bit annoying, but I'm not sure if wikidot allows for [[toc]] customization.

item.title: Re: New Guide!
item.content:encoded:
Nice!

First thing that jumps out at me is that the Table of Contents needs bullet points to break it up more clearly, but looks good otherwise.

item.title: New Guide!
item.content:encoded:
Because this is more comprehensive and organized.

Anyway, this page is maintained by me (Zyn), so if anyone has any other ideas for general (emphasis here!) reasons to contact staff, let me know.

Of course, further suggestions on wording/organization/etc are welcome to improve readability and overall look. Many, many thanks to the team captains and admins who helped out with this before it was posted. Cheers!
